text: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) closed at 17.68 on 2026-06-12, down 1.76 points (-9.05%) from the prior-session close of 19.44 on 2026-06-11, as the implied-volatility 'fear gauge' eased into Friday after a midweek spike to 22.22 on 2026-06-10.

The AAII Investor Sentiment Survey for the week ending 2026-06-11 showed bullish sentiment (individual investors expecting stocks higher over the next six months) at 30.37%, down from 36.26% the prior week and below the long-term average of 37.61%.

Bearish sentiment in the AAII Investor Sentiment Survey for the week ending 2026-06-11 jumped to 47.66% (with neutral at 21.96%), a reading AAII headlined as 'Pessimism Surges'; combined with the 30.37% bullish share this implies a bull-bear spread of roughly -17.3 percentage points, a net-pessimistic individual-investor stance.

subject.name: University of Michigan Index of Consumer Sentiment (preliminary)

text: The University of Michigan preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment for June 2026 rose to 48.9 from May's record low of 44.8 (released 2026-06-12), the first monthly improvement after consecutive declines, though the level remained historically depressed and survey commentary flagged persistent labor-market pessimism.

CNN's Fear & Greed Index - a composite of seven sentiment sub-indicators scaled 0 (extreme fear) to 100 (extreme greed) - stood at 34 on 2026-06-12, up modestly from a prior reading near 31.6 but still firmly in 'Fear' territory, signalling cautious risk appetite even as headline equity indexes closed higher for the week.
